expectations.text: Explains the rationale for accommodating resistance (matching the force/strength curve)
expectations.evidence: States: 'resistance increases as you press upward, forcing you to overcome greater load at the top.'

expectations.text: Provides a concrete example (lighter at bottom, heavier near lockout)
expectations.evidence: Describes lighter bottom / heavier lockout load curve.

expectations.text: Cites research or meta-analytic evidence showing little to no clear advantage
expectations.evidence: States: 'effect sizes are typically small—often in the 2-5% range' and 'not consistently superior.'

expectations.text: Concludes bands/chains are fine as variation tools but not reliably superior
expectations.evidence: Concludes: 'The best tool is the one you'll use correctly and consistently.'
